value_chain_position: Super-app payment and marketplace rails for Central Asia. Kaspi operates Kazakhstan's dominant fintech ecosystem (Payments + Marketplace/e-commerce + Fintech lending) on a single app and is the toll-taker on ~80% of the country's digital payment volume. load_bearing_score 5: nothing domestic routes around it; the franchise is the rail. Now extending the playbook to Turkey via Hepsiburada + a pending Rabobank Turkey banking license.
gm_trajectory: EXPANDING. Gross margin (gross profit / revenue, KZT) 64.5% (FY23) -> 62.3% (FY24) -> 73.5% (FY25) on revenue +56% YoY to 3,935B KZT. Expanding GM on rising revenue = genuine pricing power; the moat lie-detector PASSES at the gross line. The tell is at the NET line: reported net income flat (1,040B FY24 -> 1,038B FY25) as the 18% sovereign base rate and 884B KZT interest expense ate the entire top-line flow-through.
bull_thesis: Kaspi is a load-bearing monopoly (~80% of Kazakhstan's digital payments; brand 3-13x the nearest rival by category, FY25) compounding revenue ~46% over three years (3,935B KZT FY25, +56% YoY) with EXPANDING gross margins (62.3%->73.5%), 44% ROE, 42% ROIC, and a fortress balance sheet (net cash ~1,571B KZT). It trades at a deep-value multiple — roughly 7-8x earnings and EV/EBITDA ~3.4x — purely because two EXOGENOUS, MEAN-REVERTING forces have capped the reported bottom line: (1) an 18% Kazakh base rate that flat-lined GAAP net income despite +56% revenue, and (2) a self-funded Turkey/Hepsiburada build run at deliberate EBITDA breakeven. Both are turning: the National Bank cut to 17% in June 2026 (first cut since mid-2024), with EDB projecting 14% by year-end — every 100bp of normalization flows almost directly to a bottom line that is currently rate-suppressed, exactly as management has telegraphed ('top line is there... interest rates will go down and this will flow to our bottom line', Q2'25). Product velocity is real and current: Pay-by-palm hit ~0.5M users and 10% in-store penetration in one city in 90 days; e-commerce GMV +41% and value-added (advertising + delivery) revenue +73% YoY in Q1'26; Kaspi QR TPV +176%. Capital return has resumed (~64% payout dividend + buyback started early 'considering where we are in the stock'). Tencent sits on the register as a financial investor. Conditions that must hold: Kazakh rates keep falling; the sanctions/Russia overhang does not detonate; FX (KZT) is stable-to-stronger; smartphone GMV recovers as a base effect into 2026; Turkey reaches EBITDA breakeven on schedule.
bear_thesis: The reported bottom line has gone NOWHERE for a year — net income 1,040B (FY24) -> 1,038B (FY25), Q1'26 adjusted EBITDA only +9% vs revenue +31%, latest-quarter EPS -6% YoY — and the bull case leans entirely on an EXOGENOUS rate cut the company does not control in a country whose central bank 'may have to hike' and only just inched 18%->17%. The cheapness is not a gift; it is a SOVEREIGN + SANCTIONS DISCOUNT. A Culper Research short (Sept 2024) alleged card issuance to sanctioned Russian nationals, a US securities class action followed, and the entire equity is a single Kazakh-tenge, single-country bet adjacent to Russia — FX devaluation, capital controls, or a sanctions action would impair both earnings power and the USD ADS independent of operations. The 'temporary' smartphone drag has been re-litigated as temporary for four straight quarters. Turkey is a consolidated cash drag with an awkward minority/related-party structure (Hepsi minorities; Rabobank bought through the Kazakh entity, not Hepsi, because 'Hepsi doesn't have the money') and a payoff that is forward, unproven, and marketing-intensive. Payments take rate is in structural decay toward the 0.95% QR floor on the largest, most mature platform. And the cleanest internal tell: 0 open-market insider buys vs 98 sells (715,165 sh) — the CEO's 'I bought alongside Tencent' is not corroborated by net insider flow. What confirms the bear: Kazakh rates stall or reverse; KZT devalues; any sanctions enforcement or delisting noise; smartphone/GMV growth fails to re-accelerate in 2026; Turkey EBITDA stays negative past 2026.
sop_bull: ~$118-130/share. Valued by parts on normalized (rate-recovered) earnings power. Whole-company normalized net income power ~$2.4B (FY25 ~$1.98B GAAP + ~$0.4B of rate-suppressed flow-through that returns as the base rate normalizes 18%->14%). PARTS: (a) Core Kazakhstan Payments + Fintech monopoly — the cash engine, ~70% of earnings, valued at ~10x (mature monopoly toll, sovereign-discounted vs MELI/Grab/SE at 20-35x) = ~$1.68B earnings x 10 = ~$16.8B; (b) Marketplace / e-commerce (GMV +41%, VAS revenue +73%, take-rate expanding to 15.8%) — the growth engine, ~30% of earnings, valued at ~16x for its growth = ~$0.72B x 16 = ~$11.5B; (c) Turkey/Hepsiburada optionality at ~$1.0B (roughly invested capital, breakeven now, replication upside free); plus net cash ~$3.0B add-back. Sum ~$32.3B equity vs ~$15.3B today => ~$130/sh on ~190M ADS-equivalent. Even haircutting the EM multiples one notch for sovereign risk lands ~$118.
sop_bear: ~$62-70/share (theme/rate-normalization PAUSES 12-18 months). Adverse parts, rates stuck at 17-18% so NO bottom-line recovery, KZT flat, Turkey still burning, sanctions discount widens. PARTS: (a) Core Payments + Fintech on FLAT current earnings ~$1.4B at a punitive 6x sovereign/sanctions multiple = ~$8.4B; (b) Marketplace at a de-rated 9x on ~$0.55B = ~$5.0B; (c) Turkey marked to ZERO net (consolidated EBITDA drag, minority/related-party complexity) = $0; (d) net cash ~$3.0B but haircut ~30% for tenge/repatriation risk = ~$2.1B. Sum ~$15.5B => ~$62/sh; a harsher sanctions/FX scenario (tenge -20%, multiple to 5x) breaks ~$50. Downside-to-break sits modestly below the current ~$80.
sop_breakdown: Base-case reconciliation of bull/bear parts at ~525 KZT/USD on ~190M ADS-equiv shares. (a) Core Kazakhstan Payments + Fintech monopoly: ~$1.55B normalized earnings x 8.5x (mid between the bear's 6x sanctions-punished and the bull's 10x; a deep sovereign discount to EM super-app peers MELI/Grab/SE at 20-35x is WARRANTED, not stingy) = ~$13.2B. (b) Marketplace / e-commerce growth engine: ~$0.65B earnings x 13x = ~$8.5B. (c) Turkey/Hepsiburada: ~$0.6B (half of invested capital — credit for the asset and replication optionality, debit for breakeven burn + minority/related-party structure). (d) Net cash ~1,571B KZT (~$3.0B) haircut ~15% for tenge/repatriation = ~$2.55B. Sum ~$24.85B equity / 190M = ~$95/sh. Anchored against true_competitors (MELI/Grab/Sea) — KSPI's ~7-8x current and ~9-10x blended SoP multiple is a fraction of those names', explicitly reflecting single-country + tenge + sanctions concentration, which is the correct lens, not a mispricing oversight.
risk_reward: Favorable but tail-heavy. From ~$80.5: upside-to-fair ~$95 = +18%, upside-to-bull ~$130 = +61%; downside-to-bear-break ~$62 = -23%, with a fat left tail to ~$50 (-38%) on a sanctions/FX shock. Roughly 1:1.3 base downside-to-upside, improving to ~1:2.6 to the bull — but the distribution is bimodal (rate-cycle + sanctions binary), not a smooth bell. Asymmetry is real only if you can stomach the sovereign left tail.
catalyst_status: SOFT_EXTENDED. The load-bearing 'catalyst' is exogenous Kazakh rate normalization, not a dated corporate event. As of June 2026 the National Bank cut 100bp (18%->17%, first cut since mid-2024; EDB projects 14% by year-end) — the thesis-confirming turn has BEGUN but is serial, macro-driven, and reversible ('may have to hike in 2026' per ING). Smartphone-GMV recovery is the other soft, serially-deferred (4 quarters) lever. Rabobank Turkey license close 'by summer 2026' is the only semi-dated item and is regulatory/out-of-company-hands. Per the rubric this is NOT a binding asymmetric special-sit; it must NOT drive the verdict and does not — the verdict rests on theme durability, the load-bearing monopoly position, the EXPANDING gross-margin moat tell, and reinvestment economics.
peer_comps_note: true_competitors are EM super-apps — MercadoPago/MELI, Grab, Sea Limited (SE), Paytm, TBC Bank & Bank of Georgia — NOT US fintechs; KSPI's TAM is Kazakhstan + Central Asia/Turkey expansion. Those peers carry 20-35x earnings (MELI) / premium GMV multiples; KSPI trades ~7-8x. The discount is justified, not an oversight: single-country tenge concentration, sovereign/sanctions tail, and Russia proximity. Even the conservative SoP applies only a high-single-digit-to-low-teens multiple — a deep haircut to the peer set — and still lands above the current price. The relative-value lever says KSPI is structurally cheap to its growth+ROE cohort; the absolute lever says most of that gap is correct risk pricing, leaving a real but bounded margin of safety.
consensus_delta: The street is NOT looking the wrong way on the franchise — analyst targets cluster $98-114 (median ~$98-109) above the ~$80 print, and consensus correctly sees a cheap monopoly. The genuine expectations gap is on the SHAPE and TIMING of the bottom-line recovery. Two street assumptions are vulnerable: (1) the bears' implicit 'flat net income IS the run-rate' — they extrapolate the rate-suppressed FY25 GAAP line (net income dead flat 1,040B->1,038B) as steady-state, ignoring that ~$0.4B of earnings power is mechanically suppressed by the 18% base rate and returns as it normalizes (cut to 17% June 2026 already underway); (2) the bulls' implicit 'rates fall cleanly to 14%' — the EDB path is a forecast, not a guarantee, and ING flags hike risk. The real arbitrage is that the market is pricing the SANCTIONS/FX tail and the rate drag as PERMANENT impairments to value when at least the rate component is cyclical and turning. Where the street is most offside: treating ~7-8x earnings as fair for a 44%-ROE, GM-expanding monopoly because it cannot underwrite the tenge — a defensible discount, but likely an over-discount now that the rate cycle has visibly inflected.
valley_of_death: Near-term (3-9 month) hazards that hit BEFORE the rate tailwind compounds: (1) Reported earnings keep printing flat-to-down — Q1'26 adj. EBITDA +9% vs revenue +31%, latest-Q EPS -6% YoY; one cut to 17% is not enough to move the GAAP line yet, so the next 1-2 prints can disappoint headline-watchers even as the thesis improves. (2) Sanctions/litigation event risk — the Culper short and US securities class action are live; any enforcement headline, delisting noise, or new disclosure is a step-function drawdown independent of fundamentals. (3) Tenge FX — a single-currency ADS; a KZT devaluation (oil/Russia/capital-flow driven) impairs the USD price directly. (4) Turkey burn — Hepsiburada consolidated at EBITDA breakeven 'by design' is a drag on group EBITDA growth for 2026; any slippage extends the cash hump. (5) Smartphone/GMV — the 'temporary' drag has been deferred four quarters; a fifth disappointment dents the recovery narrative. (6) Payments take-rate grind toward the 0.95% floor pressures the largest platform's revenue each quarter. None of these is a solvency event (net cash ~1,571B KZT, FCF yield ~9%), but each can flush the stock toward the bear-case $62 before the rate cycle pays off.
positioning_washout: Less a forced-seller dynamic than a sentiment/sponsorship one. KSPI is NOT a yield/passive vehicle whose base liquidates on a dividend cut — the dividend was just REINSTATED (~64% payout) and a buyback started early, so income holders are being added, not shaken out. The washout risk is the opposite: it is an EM single-country ADS held by growth and EM-specialist funds, exposed to mechanical de-risking on any sanctions/sovereign headline (index exclusion, broker restriction, ADS-conversion friction) and to tenge-driven mark-downs. A clean capitulation candle most likely comes from a sanctions/FX shock that forces benchmark-sensitive EM and ESG-screened holders out at once — precisely the flush a patient buyer should wait for. Tencent on the register (financial investor) is a stabilizing, non-forced holder. Net: the decks clear on a sovereign-headline flush, not a dividend-driven one.
forcing_function: No single hard corporate date forces the re-rating; it is a sequence of exogenous + earnings checkpoints. The dominant forcing function is the KAZAKH RATE PATH: the next National Bank decisions (following the June-2026 cut to 17%) toward the EDB-forecast 14% by year-end are what convert rate-suppressed earnings power into reported net income — watch each NBK meeting. Secondary dated items: Q2/H1'26 results (the next detailed strategy call under the new format) for the first GAAP evidence of rate flow-through and a smartphone base-effect recovery; and the Rabobank Turkey license close targeted 'by summer 2026' (regulatory, out of company hands) which de-risks the Turkey optionality. The re-rate is realized as (a) rates fall AND (b) reported net income visibly inflects upward on a print — two-to-three quarters out, not imminent.
moderator_conclusion: KSPI is a genuine, load-bearing secular disruptor — the toll-taker on ~80% of Kazakhstan's digital payments, with EXPANDING gross margins (62.3%->73.5% on +56% revenue) that pass the moat lie-detector decisively, 44% ROE, 42% ROIC, net cash, and credible (if 'underlying'-flavored) guidance. The theme is durable and multi-year: this is not a capex air-pocket name, it is a monopoly compounder. The reason it sits at ~7-8x earnings is NOT franchise erosion — it is a sovereign + sanctions + tenge discount stacked on top of an 18% rate environment that has flat-lined REPORTED net income for a year. That makes it a deep-value monopoly with an exogenous, mean-reverting catalyst (rate cuts, just begun: 18%->17% June 2026) and a fat left tail (sanctions/FX/sovereign). The SoP fair value is ~$95 vs ~$80 today — a real but modest ~18% base-case margin of safety, with ~+61% to the bull if rates normalize to 14% and ~-23/-38% downside on a sanctions/FX shock. I do NOT chase it here at AGGRESSIVE: the rate inflection is one cut old, the next 1-2 GAAP prints can still disappoint, and the sanctions binary is unhedgeable. This is a WATCHLIST-FOR-CAPITULATION (B): buy the sovereign-headline or FX flush toward the bear-case $60s, where you own a 44%-ROE monopoly at ~6x into a confirmed rate-cutting cycle. Conviction 4 — high on the asset, gated by timing and tail. value_conviction 4 — judged purely on valuation vs the ~$95 SoP and the strong forensic quality (real cash, real margins, clean accounting bar the adjusted-vs-reported framing), ignoring the catalyst: the asset is cheap to its own earnings power, and the discount, while partly justified, is over-extended now that the rate cycle has inflected. The two scores happen to align here, but both rest on the value/forensic case, not on a hot catalyst.
